author: N23eos
description: Capture messages from a Telegram bot in your vault. No server required. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: n23eos
manifest.description: Capture Telegram messages, media and voice transcripts into your vault. No server required.
manifest.name: Vault Telegram Bridge

name: Vault Telegram Bridge

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What changed

- The GitHub account is renamed to lowercase `n23eos`, and this release's build-provenance attestation is signed under the new identity — it now matches the plugin directory's repository record exactly, which resolves the directory's attestation verification errors.
- All in-repo references (manifest author, banner, docs) updated to the new owner name.

No functional changes since 0.3.5. Requires Obsidian 1.13.0 or newer.

**Full changelog**: https://github.com/n23eos/vault_telegram_bridge/compare/0.3.5...0.3.6

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Highlights

**Settings tab rebuilt on Obsidian 1.13's declarative settings API.** Every option — bot token, routes, formatting, transcription, sync — now shows up in Obsidian's settings search. The disconnect button uses the proper destructive style, and the sync-interval slider shows its value inline.

**Directory audit findings resolved.**
- Attachments are located through the metadata cache instead of enumerating every file in the vault.
- UI language detection uses Obsidian's official `getLanguage()` API instead of reading localStorage.
- Invalid-input highlighting no longer relies on `!important`.
- `document.createElement`-style calls replaced with Obsidian's `createDiv()` helpers.
- Development prototypes (`spikes/`) removed from the repository.

**Release integrity.**
- Every release asset now embeds its version, so each release's build-provenance attestation covers unique bytes.
- Release notes are generated automatically from the commit history.

## Breaking

- Requires **Obsidian 1.13.0 or newer** (declarative settings API).

**Full changelog**: https://github.com/N23eos/vault_telegram_bridge/compare/0.3.1...0.3.5

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What changed

- Settings tab migrated to Obsidian 1.13's declarative settings API: every option now appears in the settings search; the disconnect button uses the destructive style.
- Attachments are located through the metadata cache instead of enumerating every vault file.
- UI language detection uses Obsidian's official getLanguage() API.
- Invalid-input highlighting no longer relies on !important.
- Development prototypes (spikes/) removed from the repository.
- Release assets now embed the version, so each release's provenance attestation covers unique bytes.

**Requires Obsidian 1.13.0 or newer.**

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's new

### Hashtag routing
Send `#idea Buy a projector` and the entry lands in `Inbox/Ideas.md` instead of the daily note. Routes are ordered rules in settings: a Telegram hashtag → a vault-relative note path (Moment date tokens like `Topics/YYYY-MM` supported) → an optional custom heading. The matched tag is removed from the saved text; other hashtags and formatting stay intact. No match — the message goes to the daily note as before.

### Voice transcription
Voice messages, audio files and round video notes can now be transcribed through any OpenAI-compatible speech-to-text endpoint — OpenAI Whisper, Groq's free tier, or a local whisper server. The transcript is written right below the audio embed. A **Check the connection** button in settings verifies your URL, key and model before the first real voice note, and the settings screen shows ready-to-paste values for Groq and OpenAI. If the provider fails, the attachment is still saved and sync continues.

### Russian locale
The full interface is now available in Russian and follows Obsidian's language setting automatically.

## Improvements and fixes

- Telegram serves voice notes as `.oga`; uploads are renamed to `.ogg` so Whisper hosts accept them (the file in your vault keeps its real name).
- Transient transcription failures (rate limits, 5xx, network) are retried on the next sync instead of permanently losing the transcript; a redelivered batch is never billed twice against your STT provider.
- An invalid route path can no longer stall syncing: the message falls back to the daily note and you are notified once.
- A route pointing at your daily note now applies the daily-note template when creating it.
- Routes with a typo in the tag survive a restart (previously they were silently deleted); invalid fields are highlighted in settings instead of being silently ignored.
- Bold/italic spanning a removed routing hashtag keeps its formatting on the surviving text.
- Hashtag matching is now locale-independent (no more surprises on Turkish-locale devices).
- Remote transcription endpoints must use `https` — plain `http` is allowed only for `localhost`, so your API key never travels unencrypted.
- A misconfigured endpoint answering HTML instead of JSON now produces a clear error message.

## Settings schema

Settings migrate automatically to schema v3 (`routes`, transcription options). No action needed.

## Build provenance

Release assets are now published with GitHub artifact attestations. Verify any asset with:

```
gh attestation verify main.js -R N23eos/vault_telegram_bridge
```

## 0.3.2

Plugin-directory audit follow-ups: locale detection now uses Obsidian's `getLanguage()` API instead of localStorage, settings UI uses `createDiv()` helpers, invalid-input highlighting no longer relies on `!important`.
releases.name: 0.3.2 — Hashtag routing, voice transcription, Russian locale
